There are **FIVE distinct primary Blob types** across different packages (the tracked-blob lifecycle), plus **seven additional Blob-shaped projection types** that are derived from blobs for specific subsystems (see \"Related Blob-shaped projection types\" below).\n\n1. **`tracking.Blob`** - 2D floor tracker (X, Z coordinates)\n2. **`tracker.Blob`** - 3D tracker with identity support (X, Y, Z coordinates + posture)\n3. **`fusion.Blob`** - Simple fusion result (X, Y, Z + confidence)\n4. **`automation.TrackedBlob`** - Automation engine blob representation\n5. **`signal.TrackedBlob`** - Fusion-to-tracking pipeline blob (X, Y, Z + velocity + identity fields)\n\n> **Line numbers verified against current `main` (2026-07-06).** Sites move by a few lines as the codebase evolves; re-locate struct {\n    ID       int\n    X        float64 // metres, room X\n    Z        float64 // metres, room Z\n    VX       float64 // m/s\n    VZ       float64 // m/s\n    Weight   float64 // localisation confidence [0..1]\n    LastSeen time.Time\n    Trail    [][2]float64\n    ukf      *UKF\n    \n    // Identity fields\n    PersonID           string\n    PersonLabel        string\n The extra fields `PersonName`,\n> `AssignedColor`, `IdentityResolved` belong only to `tracking.Blob` (2D).\n\n#### applyIdentity() - Lines 164-176\n```go\nfunc (tm *TrackManager) applyIdentity(blob *Blob, info *IdentityInfo, now time.Time) {\n    blob.PersonID = info.PersonID\n    blob.PersonLabel = info.PersonLabel\n    blob.PersonColor = info.PersonColor\n    blob.IdentityConfidence = info.IdentityConfidence\n    blob.IdentityLastSeen = now\n\n    if info.IdentitySource != \"\" {\n        blob.IdentitySource = info.IdentitySource\n    } else {\n        blob.IdentitySource = \"ble_triangulation\"\n    }\n}\n```\n- **Context**: Applying BLE identity match to blob\n- **Pattern**: Field mutation on existing blob pointer\n\n#### clearIdentity() - Lines 179-185\n```go\nfunc (tm *TrackManager) clearIdentity(blob *Blob) {\n    blob.PersonID = \"\"\n    blob.PersonLabel = \"\"\n    blob.PersonColor = \"\"\n    blob.IdentityConfidence = 0\n    blob.IdentitySource = \"\"\n}\n```\n- **Context**: Clearing identity when match expires\n- **Pattern**: Resetting identity fields to defaults\n\n---\n\n## Related Blob-shaped projection types\n\nBeyond the five primary Blob types above, the codebase defines seven additional\n`Blob*`-named structs. These are **projections** — read-only views built *from* a\ntracked blob for a specific subsystem, not new tracked entities. They do not own a\nUKF or a stable session ID. They matter for this inventory because any change to\nblob fields must be propagated to the projections that copy those fields.\n\n| Type | File:line | Built from | Purpose |\n|------|-----------|-----------|---------|\n| `tracking.BlobEvent` | `tracking/tracker.go:52` | `tracking.Blob` | Lifecycle event emitted on blob appear/disappear/re-ident (security-mode persistence, timeline) |\n| `falldetect.BlobSnapshot` | `falldetect/detector.go:69` | `tracker.Blob` | Z-trajectory input to the fall-detection state machine |\n| `explainability.BlobSnapshot` | `explainability/handler.go:95` | `tracker.Blob` | Per-link contribution / confidence breakdown for the \"Why?\" overlay |\n| `explainability.BlobExplanation` | `explainability/handler.go:27` | derived | Rendered explanation result (not a direct blob copy) |\n| `volume.BlobState` | `volume/shape.go:139` | tracked blob pos/vel | Input to spatial trigger volume point-in-volume tests (automation) |\n| `volume.BlobPos` | `volume/shape.go:1080` | blob pos | Lightweight position used inside the volume containment math |\n| `api.BlobPos` | `api/triggers.go:624` | blob pos | REST/JSON-facing position used by trigger evaluation in the API layer |\n| `replay.BlobUpdate` | `replay/types.go:303` | replay pipeline blob | Incremental blob frame pushed to the dashboard during time-travel replay |\n| `simulator.BlobResult` | `simulator/engine.go:80` | synthetic walker | Synthetic blob emitted by `spaxel-sim` (test fixture, not production) |\n\n**Construction pattern (all projections):** value-type struct literal\n(`SomeBlobSnapshot{ID: b.ID, X: b.X, ...}`) at the boundary where a tracked blob\nenters the subsystem.\n\n**Enumerated projection construction sites** (non-test, verified 2026-07-06) — every\nplace a projection is *built*. A field added to a tracked blob that a projection copies\nmust be propagated at each of these sites:\n\n| Site | Type built | Built from | Notes |\n|------|-----------|-----------|-------|\n| `cmd/mothership/main.go:2116` | `explainability.BlobSnapshot` | tracked blob | \"Why?\" overlay input; loop over all blobs |\n| `cmd/mothership/main.go:2236` | `volume.BlobPos` | tracked blob | volume-trigger evaluation; loop over all blobs |\n| `explainability/handler.go:194,255,357` | `BlobExplanation` | derived | rendered explanation result (3 return sites) |\n| `falldetect/detector.go:277` | `BlobSnapshot` | tracked blob | Z-trajectory input to fall-detect state machine |\n| `replay/pipeline.go:114,132` | `BlobUpdate` | replay blob | incremental frames pushed to dashboard during replay |\n| `simulator/engine.go:460` | `BlobResult` | synthetic walker | emitted by `spaxel-sim` (test fixture, not production) |\n| `tracking/tracker.go:173,188` | `BlobEvent` | tracked blob | appear/disappear lifecycle events |\n| `volume/shape.go:375,575,820,879` | `BlobState` | tracked blob | per-blob state in volume trigger state machine (4 sites) |\n\nRe-locate any moved site with:\n`grep -rn \"BlobSnapshot{\\|BlobState{\\|BlobPos{\\|BlobUpdate{\\|BlobEvent{\\|BlobResult{\\|BlobExplanation{\" mothership/`\n\n---\n\n## Key Findings\n\n### Files Requiring Updates for Blob Structure Changes\n\n1. **`mothership/internal/tracking/tracker.go`** (line 160)\n   - Constructor for new 2D blobs\n   \n2. **`mothership/internal/tracker/tracker.go`** (line 162)\n   - Constructor for new 3D blobs with posture\n   \n3. **`mothership/internal/fusion/fusion.go`** (line 260)\n   - Constructor for fusion result blobs\n   \n4. **`mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go`** (lines 2213, 5384)\n   - Type conversion constructors for automation and signal packages\n   \n5. **`mothership/internal/tracker/identity.go`** (lines 164-185)\n   - Identity field assignment (`applyIdentity`) and clearing (`clearIdentity`)\n\n### Creation Patterns by Type\n\n| Blob Type | Construction Method | Typical Context |\n|-----------|-------------------|-----------------|\n| `tracking.Blob` | `&Blob{}` | New track from measurement |\n| `tracker.Blob` | `&Blob{}` | New 3D track from measurement |\n| `fusion.Blob` | `Blob{}` | Peak detection result |\n| `automation.TrackedBlob` | `TrackedBlob{}` | Automation trigger evaluation |\n| `signal.TrackedBlob` | `TrackedBlob{}` | Fusion to tracking pipeline |\n\n### Identity Fields Affected\n\nWhen updating blob identity fields, these files need changes:\n- **Constructors**: `tracking/tracker.go:160` (2D) and `tracker/tracker.go:162` (3D) — initialize new fields\n- **Identity matching**: `tracker/identity.go:164-185` — `applyIdentity()` and `clearIdentity()` (operate on `tracker.Blob` only)\n- **Type conversions**: `cmd/mothership/main.go:2213` (automation) and `:5384` (signal)\n- **Field-set caveat**: `tracking.Blob` (2D) carries `PersonName`, `AssignedColor`, `IdentityResolved`; `tracker.Blob` (3D) does **not**. Adding an identity field to one type does not automatically add it to the other — both definitions must be edited deliberately.\n\n---\n\n## Recommendations\n\n1. **For adding new identity fields**: Update all 5 creation sites + the 2 identity functions\n2. **For changing blob structure**: Update the 5 type definitions, all constructors, and every projection type in the table above that copies the changed field\n3. **For identity field logic**: Focus on `tracker/identity.go` (note: only the 3D tracker has identity functions today)\n4. **For testing**: Update `fusion_test.go` fixtures when structure changes\n5. **For projection types**: Run the grep in the projection-types section to find each boundary construction site before changing copied fields\n\n---\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Status\n\n- [x] All blob creation sites are identified and listed\n- [x] Each site is documented with file path and line number\n- [x] Creation pattern is noted (constructor, literal, factory, etc.)\n- [x] Report is ready for the next bead to use\n\n**Totals:** 5 primary Blob types · 8 primary construction sites (3 direct struct literals + 3 slice/copy snapshots + 2 cross-package conversions) · 2 identity field-mutation functions · 9 additional Blob-shaped projection types · 15 projection construction sites enumerated across 7 files.\n","numLines":398,"startLine":1,"totalLines":398}}}

Position input to volume point-in-volume tests. |\n| 6 | `api.BlobPos` | `internal/api/triggers.go:624` | *(none in production — see note B)* | `{ID, X, Y, Z}` (no `PersonID`). **Test-only / dead in production.** |\n| 7 | `tracking.BlobEvent` | `internal/tracking/tracker.go:52` | `tracking.Blob` | Lifecycle event: `{BlobID, X, Z, Timestamp}`. Emitted on appear/disappear (2D tracker; X,Z only). |\n| 8 | `replay.BlobUpdate` | `internal/replay/types.go:303` | synthetic (see note D) | Incremental replay frame pushed to dashboard: `{ID, X,Y,Z, VX,VY,VZ, Weight, Posture, PersonID/Label/Color, Trail, ...}`. |\n| 9 | `simulator.BlobResult` | `internal/simulator/engine.go:80` | synthetic (see note D) | Simulated detection from `spaxel-sim`'s internal fusion grid: `{ID, Position, Confidence, Velocity, WalkerID, TrueError}`. Test fixture. |\n\n---\n\n## Construction sites (non-test, verified at HEAD `3591865`)\n\n15 non-test sites — exactly the count in `bf-4bhd`. Plus one additional boundary site\nfor falldetect (note A) that `bf-4bhd` did not enumerate.\n\n### `explainability.BlobSnapshot` (1 site)\n| Site | Built from | Context |\n|---|---|---|\n| `cmd/mothership/main.go:2206` | `signal.TrackedBlob` (`blobs` loop var) | Fusion loop builds `[]BlobSnapshot` for the \"Why?\" overlay. Not blob-derived. |\n| `internal/volume/shape.go:575` | `volume.BlobPos` (`blob.ID`) | **Only blob-derived site** — first-seen state for a blob entering evaluation. |\n| `internal/volume/shape.go:820` | sentinel `-999` | In-memory-only slot storing the inside-count. Not a tracked blob. |\n\n### `volume.BlobPos` (1 site)\n| Site | Built from | Context |\n|---|---|---|\n| `cmd/mothership/main.go:2326` | `signal.TrackedBlob` | Fusion loop builds `[]volume.BlobPos` for trigger evaluation. Copies `ID, X, Y, Z` — **does NOT copy `PersonID`** (left zero in production despite the field existing). |\n\n### `api.BlobPos` (0 production sites — see note B)\n| Site | Built from | Context |\n|---|---|---|\n| *(none)* | — | The only literal construction is `internal/api/triggers_test.go:777`. `TriggersHandler.EvaluateTriggers([]BlobPos)` at `api/triggers.go:549` is **not wired in `main.go`** (only `volumeTriggersHandler` is constructed/called). Test-only / dead code. |\n\n### `tracking.BlobEvent` (2 sites)\n| Site | Built from | Context |\n|---|---|---|\n| `internal/tracking/tracker.go:173` | `*tracking.Blob` (`b`) | `onBlobAppear` — new track created. No reassignment of `blobs` occurs\nbetween `track()` (line 2057) and the projection sites (2288/2206/2326) — confirmed by\nscoped grep. **A field added to `signal.TrackedBlob` that any of these four projections\nshould copy must be added at all four sites** (plus the `automation.TrackedBlob`\nconversion at `main.go:2303`, covered in `bf-5uzm`).\n\n---\n\n## Notes / corrections to `bf-4bhd`\n\n### Note A — `falldetect` enters via an anonymous struct, not a named blob type\n`falldetect.Detector.Update(blobs []struct{ID, X,Y,Z, VX,VY,VZ, Posture})`\n(`detector.go:249`) and `processBlob(blob struct{...})` (`detector.go:270`) take an\n**inline anonymous struct**, not a named tracked-blob type. The projection\n`falldetect.BlobSnapshot` is copied from that anonymous struct at `detector.go:277`, but\nthe **actual boundary where tracked-blob fields enter the subsystem** is\n`cmd/mothership/main.go:2288`, where `signal.TrackedBlob` is converted into the anonymous\nstruct literal inline. `bf-4bhd` enumerated only `detector.go:277`; the main.go:2288\nboundary site is added here. Any new `signal.TrackedBlob` field that fall-detection needs\nmust be threaded through both `main.go:2288` (the literal) and the anonymous-struct\nsignature at `detector.go:249/270/349/417/480/504` (every method that takes the struct).\n\n### Note B — `api.BlobPos` is test-only / dead in production\n`api.BlobPos` (`api/triggers.go:624`) and its consumer\n`TriggersHandler.EvaluateTriggers([]BlobPos)` (`api/triggers.go:549`) have **no production\ncaller**. `main.go` wires only `volumeTriggersHandler` (`api.NewVolumeTriggersHandler` at\n`main.go:1795`; `EvaluateTriggers(volumeBlobs)` at `main.go:2333`), which takes\n`[]volume.BlobPos` (`api/volume_triggers.go:782`). The sole `api.BlobPos{}` literal is in\n`triggers_test.go:777`. Treat `api.BlobPos` as a vestigial duplicate of `volume.BlobPos`\n(fewer fields — no `PersonID`); do not rely on it for production field propagation.\n\n### Note C — `volume.BlobState` is mostly not blob-derived\nOf its 4 construction sites, **only `shape.go:575` is built from a blob** (`volume.BlobPos.ID`).\n`shape.go:375` deserializes persisted trigger state from int64 timestamps;\n`shape.go:820` creates a sentinel `-999` slot that smuggles the inside-count through the\nsame map; `shape.go:879` creates a negative-keyed prediction state. `bf-4bhd`'s \"built\nfrom tracked blob\" label applies cleanly only to site 575.\n\n### Note D — `replay.BlobUpdate` and `simulator.BlobResult` are synthetic, not blob copies\nNeither is built from a tracked blob:\n- `replay.BlobUpdate` sites (`pipeline.go:114,132`) live in `generateDemoBlobs`\n  (`pipeline.go:100`) and compute positions from trig formulas (figure-8 / circular\n  walkers) — synthetic demo data for the replay preview, not a copy of a live/replayed blob.\n- `simulator.BlobResult` (`engine.go:460`) is built inside `detectBlobs()` from the\n  simulator's own fusion-grid peak (`blobPos`, `value`) and the nearest synthetic walker.\n\nA new tracked-blob field does **not** need to be propagated to these two types — they\ngenerate their own data. They are listed for completeness as blob-*shaped* projections,\nnot blob-*derived* ones.\n\n### Note E — `volume.BlobPos.PersonID` is not populated in production\n`volume.BlobPos` has a `PersonID` field (`shape.go:1082`), but the sole production\nconstruction (`main.go:2326`) copies only `ID, X, Y, Z` and leaves `PersonID = \"\"`. If a\ntrigger ever needs identity, this is the site to fix.\n\n---\n\n## Field-propagation checklist (when adding a field to `signal.TrackedBlob`)\n\nProduction projection sites that copy tracked-blob fields and would need updating:\n1. `cmd/mothership/main.go:2206` — `explainability.BlobSnapshot`\n2. `cmd/mothership/main.go:2288` — falldetect anonymous struct (+ the `detector.go` anon-struct signatures)\n3. `cmd/mothership/main.go:2326` — `volume.BlobPos`\n4. `cmd/mothership/main.go:2303` — `automation.TrackedBlob` (cross-package conversion; see `bf-5uzm`)\n\n(For `tracking.Blob` fields specifically: update `tracking.BlobEvent` at\n`tracker.go:173,188`. For `tracker.Blob` (3D): no projection in this set copies from it\ndirectly — `explainability.BlobSnapshot` and `falldetect.BlobSnapshot` are fed from\n`signal.TrackedBlob`, not `tracker.Blob`, at the production boundary.)\n\n---\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n\n- [x] Each projection type definition recorded with file:line and which tracked-blob type it is built from\n- [x] Every projection construction boundary site listed (file:line)\n- [x] Re-located moved sites with the `grep -rnE \"...\"` command (main.go sites moved +90)\n- [x] All line numbers verified against current HEAD (`3591865`, 2026-07-06)\n- [x] Findings written to `notes/bf-4wwt-projections.md`\n","numLines":206,"startLine":1,"totalLines":206}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_381fbd32df6045a7b74d8280","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\tname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\n3\tdescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\n4\tmetadata: \n5\t  node_type: memory\n6\t  type: reference\n7\t  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n8\t---\n9\t\n10\tIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n11\t\n12\t**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n13\t\n14\t**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n15\t1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n16\t2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n17\t3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n18\t   ```python\n19\t   import sqlite3, re\n20\t   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n21\t   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n22\t   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n23\t   con.execute('BEGIN')\n24\t   for rid, ts in rows:\n25\t       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n26\t           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n27\t                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n28\t   con.commit()\n29\t   ```\n30\t   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n31\t4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\n32\tNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n33\t\n34\t**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). The `sqlite3` binary IS available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-3.51.2-bin/bin/sqlite3` (not on PATH) — a one-liner repair works:\n35\t```bash\n36\tSQLITE=$(ls -d /nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3 | head -1)\n37\t$SQLITE .beads/beads.db \"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00',claimed_at) WHERE claimed_at LIKE '____-__-__ __:__:__';\"\n38\t```\n39\t(`python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"` still works as a fallback if no Nix sqlite3 is present.)\n40\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9e2059fd-1144-4a94-91b0-105254a8dd30","uuid":"d67128d8-7766-4524-ab4e-ef28c3292970","timestamp":"2026-07-06T19:38:26.205Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/.claude/projects/-home-coding-spaxel/memory/br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339.md","content":"---\nname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\ndescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: reference\n  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n---\n\nIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. WHERE rowid=?',\n                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n   con.commit()\n   ```\n   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\nNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n\n**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). The `sqlite3` binary IS available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-3.51.2-bin/bin/sqlite3` (not on PATH) — a one-liner repair works:\n```bash\nSQLITE=$(ls -d /nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3 | head -1)\n$SQLITE .beads/beads.db \"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00',claimed_at) WHERE claimed_at LIKE '____-__-__ __:__:__';\"\n```\n(`python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"` still works as a fallback if no Nix sqlite3 is present.)\n","numLines":40,"startLine":1,"totalLines":40}}}
